description: Agent-First CRM Phase 1: Three-Mode Admin Panel — (1) Inbox Queue at /admin/queue (inbox watcher, reply classification, draft responses, approval UI), (2) Active Pipeline at /admin/pipeline (all active convos, who owes what, days silent, Smartlead campaign + step), (3) Prospect Discovery at /admin/prospects (signal scanning from knowledge graph, queue new leads into Smartlead).

userStories.title: Create reply_queue table migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the reply_queue table created in Supabase with all columns, constraints, RLS, and indexes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create reply_queue table with columns: id (uuid PK), lead_id (uuid FK nullable to leads), inbox (text NOT NULL CHECK claru/moonvalley), sender_email (text NOT NULL), sender_name (text), gmail_message_id (text NOT NULL), gmail_thread_id (text), received_at (timestamptz NOT NULL), subject (text), body_full (text), body_snippet (text GENERATED ALWAYS AS left(body_full, 500) STORED), classification (text NOT NULL CHECK IN interested/question/requirements/not_interested/bounce/internal_forward/unsubscribe/unknown), draft_response (text), draft_status (text NOT NULL DEFAULT pending CHECK IN pending/approved/dismissed/sent/snoozed/needs_manual_draft), snoozed_until (timestamptz), approved_at (timestamptz), sent_at (timestamptz), created_at (timestamptz DEFAULT now()), updated_at (timestamptz DEFAULT now())
Add composite unique constraint: ALTER TABLE reply_queue ADD CONSTRAINT reply_queue_inbox_message_unique UNIQUE (inbox, gmail_message_id)
Add partial index: CREATE INDEX idx_reply_queue_pending ON reply_queue (draft_status, received_at DESC) WHERE draft_status IN ('pending', 'snoozed', 'needs_manual_draft')
Enable RLS with no policies (default deny): ALTER TABLE reply_queue ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY
Add updated_at trigger: CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON reply_queue FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column()
Migration file created at supabase/migrations/ with timestamped name
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Service-role key bypasses RLS. No policies = only service-role client can read/write.

userStories.title: Create lead_crm_data table migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a separate lead_crm_data table for admin-only CRM fields that must stay hidden from portal users.
acceptanceCriteria: Create lead_crm_data table with columns: lead_id (uuid PK FK to leads), type (text CHECK demand/supply DEFAULT demand), icp_score (int CHECK 1-10), last_touch_at (timestamptz), last_touch_by (text), thread_state (text CHECK cold/warm/hot/negotiating/closed/dead DEFAULT cold), smartlead_campaign_id (bigint), created_at (timestamptz DEFAULT now()), updated_at (timestamptz DEFAULT now())
Enable RLS with no policies: ALTER TABLE lead_crm_data ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY
Add updated_at trigger using existing update_updated_at_column() function
Migration file created at supabase/migrations/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Separate from leads table because leads has authenticated user RLS policy — portal users must NOT see CRM fields. Note: US-018 adds pipeline columns via a separate ALTER TABLE migration.

userStories.title: Create inbox_sync_state table migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an inbox_sync_state checkpoint table for the agent watermark.
acceptanceCriteria: Create inbox_sync_state table with columns: inbox (text PK CHECK claru/moonvalley), last_processed_at (timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT 1970-01-01), last_message_id (text), last_error (text nullable), updated_at (timestamptz DEFAULT now())
Enable RLS with no policies
Add updated_at trigger using existing update_updated_at_column() function
Seed rows: INSERT INTO inbox_sync_state (inbox) VALUES ('claru'), ('moonvalley')
Migration file created at supabase/migrations/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: last_error is set when Gmail MCP fails, cleared on next successful run. Agent reads last_processed_at to derive after:YYYY/MM/DD Gmail query.

userStories.title: Create assertAdmin helper and ADMIN_EMAILS config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable assertAdmin() helper that checks the current Supabase session user against an ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/auth/admin.ts with assertAdmin() function
assertAdmin reads Supabase session via createSupabaseServerClient(), gets user email, checks against ADMIN_EMAILS

ADMIN_EMAILS parsed from ADMIN_EMAILS env var (NO NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix — server-only, must NOT be exposed to client bundle), comma-separated, defaults to john@claru.ai,john@moonvalley.com,chad@claru.ai
assertAdmin throws Error('unauthorized') if user email not in list or no session
Export isAdmin(email: string): boolean helper for use in Server Components
Add ADMIN_EMAILS=john@claru.ai,john@moonvalley.com,chad@claru.ai to .env.example with comment: '# Admin email allowlist — server-only, do NOT use NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ADMIN_EMAILS must NOT have NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix — that would expose the admin email list in the client JS bundle to all portal visitors. Used in every Server Action AND in portal layout for PortalNav admin check. The isAdmin(email) helper is called server-side only.

userStories.title: Update portal layout to pass isAdmin and add 3-tab admin nav
userStories.description: As an admin, I need the portal layout to detect my admin status and render three admin nav tabs: Queue (with pending badge), Pipeline, and Prospects.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/portal/layout.tsx to fetch Supabase user session server-side via createSupabaseServerClient()
Import isAdmin helper from src/lib/auth/admin.ts — reads ADMIN_EMAILS env var (server-only, no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix)
Fetch pending queue count for badge: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM reply_queue WHERE draft_status IN ('pending', 'needs_manual_draft') using service-role client
Pass isAdmin boolean and pendingCount as props to PortalNav component

PortalNav accepts isAdmin and pendingCount props and renders three admin tabs only when isAdmin=true: Queue (link /admin/queue, pending badge), Pipeline (link /admin/pipeline), Prospects (link /admin/prospects)
Non-admin users see no admin tabs in nav
Existing portal functionality (data catalog, prospect pages) unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Current layout.tsx is a pure wrapper with no session fetching — this adds createSupabaseServerClient() + getUser() call. ADMIN_EMAILS is server-only (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix). Client component PortalNav receives isAdmin: boolean as a prop, never the email list itself.

userStories.title: Create queue Server Actions
userStories.description: As an admin, I need server actions to approve, dismiss, and snooze queue items.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/queue/actions.ts with Server Actions

approveItem(id: string): calls assertAdmin(), updates draft_status to 'approved', sets approved_at to now(), ALSO updates lead_crm_data SET waiting_on='them', last_touch_at=now() for the linked lead_id (skip crm update if lead_id IS NULL), revalidates /admin/queue
dismissItem(id: string): calls assertAdmin(), updates draft_status to 'dismissed', revalidates /admin/queue
snoozeItem(id: string, until: Date): calls assertAdmin(), updates draft_status to 'snoozed', sets snoozed_until, revalidates /admin/queue
updateDraft(id: string, newDraft: string): calls assertAdmin(), updates draft_response text, revalidates /admin/queue
All actions use createSupabaseAdminClient() (service-role key), NOT user session client
All actions call assertAdmin() as first line — throws unauthorized for non-admins
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: approveItem must update lead_crm_data.waiting_on='them' so the Pipeline mode shows the correct ball-in-their-court status after approval. Service-role client bypasses RLS.

userStories.title: Create queue page Server Component
userStories.description: As an admin, I need a /admin/queue page that shows pending reply queue items.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/queue/page.tsx as Server Component
Page checks admin status: if not admin, call notFound()

Fetch pending queue items using service-role client: SELECT * FROM reply_queue WHERE draft_status IN ('pending', 'needs_manual_draft') OR (draft_status = 'snoozed' AND snoozed_until < now()) ORDER BY received_at DESC
Join with leads table to get lead name and company
Check inbox_sync_state for last_error — pass to client for error banner
Pass items + sync state to QueueList client component
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Data fetching happens server-side with service-role key. Client component handles interactivity.

userStories.title: Create QueueList client component
userStories.description: As an admin, I need an interactive list of queue items that auto-refreshes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/queue/QueueList.tsx as 'use client' component
Renders list of QueueItem components
Auto-polls via router.refresh() every 60 seconds to get fresh server data
Shows inbox sync error banner if inbox_sync_state.last_error is non-null: 'Gmail sync failed at [timestamp]: [error message]'
Empty state: 'No queue items — you're caught up' with checkmark icon
Matches existing portal design system (dark theme, monospace accents, sage green accent)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Design system: bg #0a0908, card #121210, border #2a2a28, accent #92B090 (sage green), monospace JetBrains Mono.

userStories.title: Create QueueItem client component
userStories.description: As an admin, I need each queue item to show the email details, classification, editable draft, and action buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/queue/QueueItem.tsx as 'use client' component
Shows: sender name + email, company name (from lead), classification badge (colored by type), received_at relative time, email subject, body_snippet
Draft response in editable monospace textarea with auto-resize
For needs_manual_draft items: show 'Needs manual draft' label, empty textarea ready for input
For lead_id=NULL items: show 'Unmatched sender — assign to a lead' prompt
Action buttons: Approve (calls approveItem, copies draft_response to clipboard), Dismiss (calls dismissItem), Snooze (calls snoozeItem with 24h default)
Classification badge colors: interested=green, question=blue, requirements=purple, not_interested=gray, bounce=red, internal_forward=yellow, unknown=orange
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Approve copies to clipboard for pasting into Spark/Gmail. Phase 1 does not send emails automatically.

userStories.title: Add loading skeleton and error boundary for queue page
userStories.description: As an admin, I need loading and error states for the queue page.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/queue/loading.tsx with skeleton cards matching QueueItem layout
Create src/app/admin/queue/error.tsx with error boundary that shows error message and retry button
Skeleton uses pulse animation matching portal design system
Error boundary calls reset() on retry click
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add pending count badge to PortalNav Queue link
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see how many items need attention without navigating to the queue page.
acceptanceCriteria: PortalNav Queue link shows count badge with number of pending + needs_manual_draft items
Badge count fetched server-side in portal layout.tsx via service-role client: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM reply_queue WHERE draft_status IN ('pending', 'needs_manual_draft')
Badge only renders when count > 0
Badge styled as small circle with count, sage green background, positioned top-right of Queue link
Pass pendingCount as prop alongside isAdmin to PortalNav
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 (PortalNav already accepts isAdmin prop and all three admin tabs).

userStories.title: Attio CSV backfill migration script
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a one-time migration script that seeds lead_crm_data from an Attio CSV export.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/backfill-attio-crm.ts
Script reads Attio export CSV (path as CLI arg)
For each Attio contact: match to existing leads table by email, insert lead_crm_data row with type=demand/supply (from Attio tags), thread_state based on Attio status

Also query Smartlead API GET /api/v1/campaigns/lead?email={email}&api_key={SMARTLEAD_API_KEY} to find the lead's Smartlead campaign_id and populate smartlead_lead_id (Smartlead's internal lead record ID) for each matched lead
Smartlead API key read from SMARTLEAD_API_KEY env var (passed as ?api_key= query param)
Script is idempotent: uses INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (lead_id) DO UPDATE
Logs: matched N leads, skipped N (no match), created N lead_crm_data rows, found N Smartlead lead IDs
Uses service-role key from .env.local
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Run BEFORE agent starts. Also populates smartlead_lead_id needed for US-020 Smartlead sync. Smartlead auth: all API calls append ?api_key={SMARTLEAD_API_KEY} as a query param (not a header).

userStories.title: Feasibility spike: Remote Task reads Gmail and writes Supabase
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to validate that Claude Code Remote Tasks can access Gmail MCP and write to Supabase before building the agent loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a test Remote Task (via /schedule) that: queries Gmail via Google Workspace MCP for 3 recent emails, writes a test row to inbox_sync_state, reads it back
Document whether Google Workspace MCP is available in Remote Task environment
Test Composio GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS action for john@moonvalley.com from Remote Task
If Gmail MCP fails: document fallback plan (Edge Function + googleapis npm package)
If Composio fails: document REST API fallback (POST /api/v1/actions/GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS/execute)
Test Smartlead API access from Remote Task: GET /api/v1/campaigns/lead?email=test@example.com&api_key={SMARTLEAD_API_KEY} — verify the SMARTLEAD_API_KEY env var is accessible from Remote Task env
Results documented in tasks/spike-remote-task-results.md
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BLOCKING for US-014 (inbox reader) and US-020 (Smartlead sync). SMARTLEAD_API_KEY must be in Remote Task environment — verify this. If Smartlead API is inaccessible from Remote Task, US-020 must use Smartlead CLI instead.

userStories.title: Agent: inbox reader with date-based checkpoint
userStories.description: As the system, I need the agent to read both inboxes using date-based filters and checkpoint correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/agent/inbox-reader.ts (or equivalent agent prompt/script)
Reads inbox_sync_state.last_processed_at for each inbox
Queries claru.ai inbox via Google Workspace MCP with query: after:YYYY/MM/DD (derived from last_processed_at)
For each message: calls gmail_get_message_details to fetch full body
Queries moonvalley.com inbox via Composio (or REST API fallback per spike results)
Inserts into reply_queue with ON CONFLICT ON CONSTRAINT reply_queue_inbox_message_unique DO NOTHING
After processing: advances inbox_sync_state.last_processed_at to newest message timestamp
On success: clears inbox_sync_state.last_error to NULL
On failure: sets inbox_sync_state.last_error with error message
When a new reply is processed: updates lead_crm_data SET waiting_on='us' for the matched lead_id (if not null)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on spike results (US-013). Sets waiting_on='us' on new replies so Pipeline mode immediately shows ball is in our court.

userStories.title: Agent: sender matching and reply classification
userStories.description: As the system, I need incoming emails matched to leads and classified by intent.
acceptanceCriteria: Match sender to leads table by email address (exact match on email column)
If no exact match: try domain match (extract domain from sender_email, find leads with same domain)
If no match: set lead_id=NULL, classification='unknown'
Classify reply intent into one of: interested, question, requirements, not_interested, bounce, internal_forward, unsubscribe, unknown
Classification uses email body content, subject, and sender context
Write classification to reply_queue.classification column
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Classification is done by Claude in the Remote Task — this is LLM-based, not rule-based.

userStories.title: Agent: draft response generation per GTM playbook
userStories.description: As the system, I need draft responses generated following the GTM playbook rules.
acceptanceCriteria: For non-bounce, non-unsubscribe replies: generate a draft response
Draft follows GTM playbook rules from gtm/strategy/messaging.md: no em-dashes, founder posture, appropriate CTA
For demand-side interested replies: include relevant dataset/clip context from clips table
For supply-side replies: use partnership tone per supply-side CTA rules
Draft written to reply_queue.draft_response column
If draft generation fails: set draft_status='needs_manual_draft', draft_response=NULL
Update lead_crm_data.last_touch_at and thread_state based on classification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GTM playbook is the agent's rulebook. Draft quality is critical — this is what the founder reviews.

userStories.title: Schedule inbox reader as recurring Remote Task (every 30 min)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the inbox reader agent loop scheduled to run every 30 minutes autonomously.
acceptanceCriteria: Configure Claude Code Remote Task (or /schedule cron) running every 30 minutes
Task prompt includes: read both inboxes, match senders, classify, draft responses, write to reply_queue, update waiting_on
Task has access to: Google Workspace MCP, Composio, Supabase service-role key, GTM playbook files
Task runs independently without requiring a local Claude Code session
First run processes all recent emails and populates reply_queue
Document the scheduled task configuration in tasks/agent-schedule.md
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Inbox reader runs every 30 min. Smartlead sync (US-020) runs every 1 hour as a separate task. Signal scanner (US-021) runs daily.

userStories.title: Add Active Pipeline columns to lead_crm_data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need four new columns on lead_crm_data to power the Pipeline mode: smartlead_lead_id, waiting_on, smartlead_campaign_name, smartlead_sequence_step.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a new migration file (after US-002 migration) with ALTER TABLE lead_crm_data ADD COLUMN
ADD COLUMN smartlead_lead_id bigint — Smartlead internal lead ID for API queries
ADD COLUMN waiting_on text CHECK (waiting_on IN ('us', 'them', 'unknown')) DEFAULT 'unknown'
ADD COLUMN smartlead_campaign_name text — human-readable campaign name, synced hourly by US-020
ADD COLUMN smartlead_sequence_step int — which step (1, 2, 3) of the sequence they're currently on, synced hourly by US-020
Migration file created at supabase/migrations/ with timestamp after US-002
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No smartlead_next_touch_at column — Smartlead's public API does not expose per-lead next scheduled send date. Instead show campaign_name + sequence_step in the Pipeline UI. waiting_on lifecycle: inbox reader sets 'us' on new reply, approveItem sets 'them' on approval. All new columns nullable — existing rows from Attio backfill start with NULLs, which is correct.

userStories.title: Create prospect_signals table migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a prospect_signals table to store flagged opportunities from the signal scanner agent.
acceptanceCriteria: Create prospect_signals table with columns: id (uuid PK), company_name (text NOT NULL), contact_name (text), contact_email (text), signal_type (text NOT NULL CHECK hiring/funding/product_launch/gtm_file/news), signal_description (text NOT NULL), source_url (text), status (text CHECK new/queued/skipped DEFAULT new), created_at (timestamptz DEFAULT now()), updated_at (timestamptz DEFAULT now())
Add unique constraint: ALTER TABLE prospect_signals ADD CONSTRAINT prospect_signals_company_signal_unique UNIQUE (company_name, signal_type)
Use ON CONFLICT ON CONSTRAINT prospect_signals_company_signal_unique DO UPDATE SET updated_at = now() (NOT DO NOTHING) — ensures signal refreshes if re-scanned, and allows status to be reset manually
Enable RLS with no policies: ALTER TABLE prospect_signals ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY
Add updated_at trigger: CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at BEFORE UPDATE ON prospect_signals FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column()
Migration file created at supabase/migrations/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DO UPDATE SET updated_at (not DO NOTHING) allows re-scanning to refresh signals and enables a future 'reset to new' admin action. updated_at is needed because status mutates (new→queued/skipped) and we need an audit trail. contact_name and contact_email are nullable — scanner populates them via LLM extraction from the company file body; admin can also fill in manually from the UI.

userStories.title: Smartlead sync agent (hourly) — campaign name and sequence step
userStories.description: As the system, I need an hourly agent that syncs each active lead's Smartlead campaign name and sequence step into lead_crm_data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/agent/smartlead-sync.ts (or equivalent agent prompt/script)
Reads SMARTLEAD_API_KEY from env (server-only var, added in US-004's .env.example). All API calls append ?api_key={SMARTLEAD_API_KEY} as query param (Smartlead's auth mechanism)
Reads all leads from lead_crm_data WHERE thread_state NOT IN ('cold', 'dead') AND smartlead_lead_id IS NOT NULL

For each lead: calls Smartlead API GET /api/v1/campaigns/lead?email={lead_email}&api_key={key} to get campaign assignment and sequence step number. BEFORE IMPLEMENTING: inspect the actual API response JSON for a test lead (e.g., curl this endpoint with a real lead email) and confirm the exact field name for sequence step — common field names in Smartlead responses include 'seq_number', 'sequence_number', 'email_step_seq_number'. Use the actual field name from the response, not an assumed name.
Updates lead_crm_data SET smartlead_campaign_name = [campaign.name from API response], smartlead_sequence_step = [confirmed sequence step field name from API response]
Rate limiting: 200ms delay between each API call. On HTTP 429 response: log 'rate limited', abort the remaining leads for this run (not just skip one lead), write error to a log table or stdout
On non-429 error for a single lead: log error, skip that lead, continue with others
Scheduled as a separate Remote Task running every 1 hour
SMARTLEAD_API_KEY added to .env.example with comment: '# Smartlead API key — get from Smartlead dashboard → Settings → API. Used as ?api_key= query param.'
Configuration documented in tasks/agent-schedule.md
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-013 spike confirming Smartlead API and SMARTLEAD_API_KEY access from Remote Task env. Note: Smartlead API does NOT expose per-lead next scheduled send date — do not attempt to fetch or display it. Campaign name + sequence step is what's available and is sufficient for the Pipeline view.

userStories.title: Signal scanner agent (daily at 8am ET)
userStories.description: As the system, I need a daily agent that scans gtm/knowledge-graph/companies/ for high-ICP companies not yet in the leads table and writes them to prospect_signals.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/agent/signal-scanner.ts (or equivalent agent prompt/script)
Reads all .md files in gtm/knowledge-graph/companies/
Parses frontmatter: extracts icp_score (0-100 scale — note: this is different from lead_crm_data.icp_score which uses 1-10 scale), company name (from 'name' field), domain
For each file with icp_score >= 80 (0-100 scale, meaning high-confidence ICP targets): check if company domain already in leads table (domain match on lead email domain)

If NOT in leads: extract primary contact using LLM — read the '## Key People' table from the file body, extract the first row marked as the planned contact (highest confidence email), extract contact name and email

Insert into prospect_signals with signal_type='gtm_file', contact_name and contact_email from LLM extraction (nullable if extraction fails), signal_description = 1-2 sentence summary of why this company is a target (extracted from file overview)
Uses ON CONFLICT ON CONSTRAINT prospect_signals_company_signal_unique DO UPDATE SET updated_at = now() — safe to run multiple times
Scheduled as a Remote Task running daily at 8am ET (cron: 0 13 * * *)
Configuration documented in tasks/agent-schedule.md
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL: icp_score in knowledge graph files uses 0-100 scale (e.g., Sama=95, iMerit=92). Threshold is >= 80, not >= 8. The lead_crm_data.icp_score column uses 1-10 scale — these are DIFFERENT scales, do not confuse them. Contact info (name, email) is NOT in frontmatter — it is in the '## Key People' markdown table in the file body. Use LLM extraction, not frontmatter parsing, for contact fields.

userStories.title: Create pipeline page Server Component
userStories.description: As an admin, I need a /admin/pipeline page that shows all active leads sorted by days silent.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/pipeline/page.tsx as Server Component
Page checks admin status: if not admin, call notFound()

Fetch active leads using service-role client with this query: SELECT lcd.*, l.email, l.name, l.company, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - lcd.last_touch_at)) / 86400, 9999) AS days_silent FROM lead_crm_data lcd JOIN leads l ON lcd.lead_id = l.id WHERE lcd.thread_state IN ('warm', 'hot', 'negotiating') ORDER BY days_silent DESC
COALESCE ensures leads with NULL last_touch_at (never contacted) appear at top with days_silent=9999 and render as 'Never contacted' in the UI — do NOT omit them

Fetch last 3 reply_queue events per lead using a SINGLE CTE with ROW_NUMBER() window function — no separate per-lead queries and no programmatic IN list: WITH active_leads AS (SELECT lead_id FROM lead_crm_data WHERE thread_state IN ('warm','hot','negotiating')), ranked AS (SELECT rq.*, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY rq.lead_id ORDER BY rq.received_at DESC) AS rn FROM reply_queue rq JOIN active_leads al ON rq.lead_id = al.lead_id) SELECT * FROM ranked WHERE rn <= 3. This is one round trip, no IN list construction.
Pass leads + activity data to PipelineList client component
Add loading.tsx and error.tsx for the pipeline route
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: COALESCE is critical — NULL days_silent for never-contacted leads breaks client-side sort (NaN comparisons) and the red-highlight logic. 9999 as sentinel value pushes them to the top. ROW_NUMBER window function is specified explicitly to prevent N+1 query implementation.

userStories.title: Create PipelineList and PipelineRow client components
userStories.description: As an admin, I need an interactive pipeline table with sortable columns, demand/supply filter, and expandable rows showing activity history.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/pipeline/PipelineList.tsx as 'use client' component

Renders sortable table with columns: Contact (name + email), Company, Type (demand/supply badge), Thread State (colored badge), Days Silent (shows 'Never contacted' if days_silent=9999; highlights red if days_silent > 3), Waiting On (US badge in orange / THEM badge in gray / UNKNOWN badge), Smartlead (shows '[campaign_name] — Step [N]' if set, or '—' if not in campaign)
Default sort: days_silent DESC. User can click column headers to re-sort client-side
Filter toggle buttons: All / Demand / Supply — filters by type field
Create src/app/admin/pipeline/PipelineRow.tsx as 'use client' component
Clicking a row expands to show last 3 activity events (subject line, date, classification badge)

If waiting_on='us' AND lead has a pending queue item (draft_status IN pending/needs_manual_draft — NOT snoozed): show 'Draft response →' link to /admin/queue#[item-id]. If the item is snoozed: show 'Snoozed until [date]' instead of the link
Matches portal design system (dark theme, monospace, sage green accent)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Smartlead column shows campaign_name + sequence_step (not a date). 'Draft response' link must check draft_status — do not show the link if the queue item is snoozed, as the item won't appear on the queue page until snoozed_until passes.

userStories.title: Create pipeline Server Actions
userStories.description: As an admin, I need server actions to update thread state and waiting_on directly from the Pipeline view.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/pipeline/actions.ts with Server Actions
updateThreadState(leadId: string, state: 'cold' | 'warm' | 'hot' | 'negotiating' | 'closed' | 'dead'): calls assertAdmin(), updates lead_crm_data.thread_state, revalidates /admin/pipeline
updateWaitingOn(leadId: string, value: 'us' | 'them' | 'unknown'): calls assertAdmin(), updates lead_crm_data.waiting_on, revalidates /admin/pipeline
All actions use createSupabaseAdminClient() (service-role key)
All actions call assertAdmin() as first line
PipelineRow renders a thread state dropdown that calls updateThreadState on change
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Admin can manually override thread_state and waiting_on from the pipeline view without opening the queue.

userStories.title: Create prospect discovery page Server Component
userStories.description: As an admin, I need a /admin/prospects page that shows new prospect signals from the signal scanner.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/prospects/page.tsx as Server Component
Page checks admin status: if not admin, call notFound()
Fetch new signals: SELECT * FROM prospect_signals WHERE status = 'new' ORDER BY created_at DESC
Pass signals to ProspectList client component — do NOT fetch Smartlead campaigns in the Server Component (this would block the entire page if Smartlead API is slow or down)

ProspectList fetches the Smartlead campaign list client-side on component mount (GET /api/v1/campaigns?limit=50&status=active&api_key={key} via a Next.js API route at /api/admin/smartlead-campaigns). This keeps Smartlead availability separate from page rendering

Create /api/admin/smartlead-campaigns/route.ts: Next.js Route Handler. IMPORTANT: Route Handlers do NOT automatically have session context — unlike Server Components. Must explicitly plumb: (1) import { createSupabaseServerClient } from src/lib/supabase/server, (2) call createSupabaseServerClient() with the request cookies, (3) call assertAdmin() with that client — throw 401 if not admin. Then proxy GET /api/v1/campaigns?limit=50&status=active&api_key={SMARTLEAD_API_KEY} to Smartlead and return the campaign list as JSON. Reads SMARTLEAD_API_KEY from env (server-only var).
Add loading.tsx and error.tsx for the prospects route
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Smartlead campaign fetch moved client-side to prevent Smartlead API latency/downtime from blocking the entire Server Component page render. The signals (from Supabase) load instantly; the campaign dropdown in ProspectCard can show a loading state independently.

userStories.title: Create ProspectList, ProspectCard components and prospect Server Actions
userStories.description: As an admin, I need interactive prospect signal cards with Queue in Smartlead and Skip actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/prospects/ProspectList.tsx as 'use client' component — renders list of ProspectCard components, fetches campaign list from /api/admin/smartlead-campaigns on mount, passes campaigns to each card
Create src/app/admin/prospects/ProspectCard.tsx as 'use client' component

ProspectCard shows: company name, contact name + email (or 'Contact unknown — fill in before queuing' if NULL), signal type badge (hiring=green, funding=blue, gtm_file=sage, news=gray), signal description, source URL link, created_at date
If contact_email is NULL: show an inline form with name + email fields that admin fills before queuing
'Queue in Smartlead' shows a campaign dropdown (from ProspectList campaigns prop) then calls queueInSmartlead Server Action
Create src/app/admin/prospects/actions.ts

queueInSmartlead(signalId: string, campaignId: string, contactEmail: string, contactName: string, companyName: string): calls assertAdmin(), validates contactEmail is non-empty (throw if missing), calls Smartlead API POST /api/v1/leads to add the contact to the campaign (auth: ?api_key={SMARTLEAD_API_KEY}), creates a leads table row in Supabase using INSERT INTO leads (name, email, company) VALUES (contactName, contactEmail, companyName) ON CONFLICT (email) DO UPDATE SET email=EXCLUDED.email RETURNING id — using DO UPDATE SET email=EXCLUDED.email (a no-op update) instead of DO NOTHING ensures the RETURNING id clause always yields the lead_id whether the row was inserted or already existed; capture lead_id from that result, ALSO INSERT INTO lead_crm_data (lead_id, type, thread_state, smartlead_campaign_id) VALUES ([lead_id], 'demand', 'cold', [campaignId]) ON CONFLICT (lead_id) DO NOTHING — this ensures the new lead appears in the Pipeline view and Smartlead sync from day 1, updates prospect_signals.status='queued', revalidates /admin/prospects
skipSignal(signalId: string): calls assertAdmin(), updates prospect_signals.status='skipped', revalidates /admin/prospects
Empty state: 'No new prospect signals — scanner runs daily at 8am ET'
Matches portal design system
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: queueInSmartlead requires non-null contactEmail — validated server-side. leads table INSERT uses contactName as name (NOT NULL on leads table), with companyName as company. If contact info was NULL on the signal, admin fills it in via the inline form before the action is callable. SMARTLEAD_API_KEY used as query param per Smartlead auth pattern.
